prestazioni: Replace the stock 1,991cc Standard SC engine (engine code: SC) with a fully balanced and blueprinted SC block bored to 87mm (2,138cc) using a forged crankshaft from Laystall Engineering (78mm stroke) paired with Mahle Motorsport forged pistons (9.5:1 compression ratio) and Carrillo H-beam connecting rods for 200+ hp on methanol fuel with a custom 45mm Weber IDF carburetor setup

Install a bespoke 3-into-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold by Janspeed Engineering with 1.75" primary tubes merging into a 2.5" collector, paired with a free-flowing Burgess silencer (part no. 30012) for optimized scavenging and a 15% power increase over stock

Upgrade the stock 3-speed gearbox to a close-ratio 4-speed unit from a Triumph TR3A (gearbox code: A-type) with a Quaife ATB differential (25% preload) and a lightweight aluminum flywheel from Racetorations Ltd. (12.5 lbs) for faster rev matching and reduced rotational mass
estetica: Replace the stock steel body with a hand-formed aluminum widebody kit by Fibretech Motorsport (TR2-specific, part no. FT-TR2-WB) featuring flared front and rear fenders (2.5" wider than stock) with integrated rivet-style fasteners and a one-piece aluminum hood with a functional NACA duct for carburetor cooling

Install a period-correct Le Mans-style hardtop by Triumph Sports Six Club (TSSC) with a Perspex rear window and a custom roll bar integrated into the B-pillars, finished in British Racing Green (BS381C-224) with gold pinstriping by Classic Coachlines

Fit 15x7" Minilite magnesium wheels (offset ET15) wrapped in Avon CR6ZZ tires (185/60R15 front, 205/60R15 rear) with polished aluminum center caps and stainless steel locking wheel nuts by Talon Motorsport, paired with a custom front splitter and rear diffuser by Kamei (TR2-specific, part no. KAM-TR2-SD)
